config.rationale: User requested to evaluate MTP (NEXTN speculative decoding) on SGLang and language-model-only mode on vLLM. Since the last experiment was a failed SGLang config, I'll run the SGLang MTP test first. The user provided the exact command line, so I'll translate it to the configuration schema. This will test the built-in MTP layers for speculative decoding with prefix caching enabled.

smoke_tests.json_mode_detail: No content in response (reasoning model may not support JSON mode)

llm_commentary: The latest SGLang experiment with NEXTN speculative decoding achieved nearly identical performance to the previous best SGLang configuration (395.3 vs 395.6 throughput, 119.5 vs 120.4ms TTFT), indicating that MTP speculation provided minimal benefit. While it maintains good latency under 120ms, it remains far behind the vLLM leader's exceptional 2698.1 throughput with 66.6ms latency.
docker_command: docker run --name bench-sglang-4bc2cbec4fc0 --gpus all --shm-size 16g --network host -v /root/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface -d -e SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2=1 lmsysorg/sglang:latest python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tp-size 1 --dtype auto --enable-metrics --mem-fraction-static 0.9 --context-length 262144 --schedule-policy lpm --mamba-scheduler-strategy extra_buffer --speculative-eagle-topk 1 --speculative-algorithm NEXTN --speculative-num-steps 3 --reasoning-parser qwen3 --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder
